name: sea
description: Senior Engineering Architect — designs hardened architectures, audits brownfield code for primitive gaps, and decomposes designs into atomic Work Packages with Red-Green-Blue verification. /sea:probe v0.9.0 uses a Python orchestrator (scripts/probe.py) to invoke ast-grep + lizard + scc + git plus optional tools (jscpd, ts-prune, vulture, deadcode, dependency-cruiser, import-linter, detect-secrets) across 17 deterministic phases — 15 per-workspace + 2 repo-wide (Deployment Topology with first-class Sulis manifest support, Credential Scanning hash-only / baseline-aware). Polyglot 4-stage workspace enumeration adds auxiliary packages, code-bearing dirs, and deployment-only dirs alongside the existing monorepo manifests. Outputs structured JSON + Markdown + a self-contained navigable HTML report. 88+ unit + integration tests; no LLM fallback. Every WP carries a change primitive (22-primitive Minto-pyramid catalogue). v0.11.6: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). Lead with outcomes; concrete over abstract; no mechanism narration. v0.11.7: adds FE-11 (Inference Over Interrogation) citation — infer from existing context before asking the founder; never relay a specialist's 'open questions' verbatim. v0.12.0: adds /code-review skill — a PR-scoped umbrella that runs three lenses in parallel (architecture / security / general quality) against a PR number, branch ref, or commit range. Scans the diff plus a one-hop neighbour ring (callers, callees, shared-import modules) capped at 20 files; findings in the diff can block merge, findings in the neighbour ring are surfaced as exposure (downgraded one severity, never merge-blocking). Invokes /sulis-security:codebase-assess in Quick mode for the security lens. Outputs a single merged report at .architecture/{project}/code-reviews/PR-{number}-{date}.md plus draft Hardening Deltas with source: code-review:PR-NNN. Advisory only — never posts to the PR, never sets status checks, never auto-blocks. v0.14.0: adds CR-09 (PR Hygiene Application) to the Code Review Standard and rewires /code-review to apply the new PR Hygiene Standard (PH-01..PH-08) published by SRD v1.21.0. Closes user feedback that

was a missing indicator. The hygiene check runs before lens dispatch, computes a deterministic signal table (diff size, file count, commit-type spread, module fan-out, migration count, schema/IDL count, infra-file count, lock-file ratio, secret-pattern hits, new-source-without-test count), and feeds CR-06 verdict auto-downgrade rule 4 — PH-03 high findings (4+ migrations, plaintext secret pattern) → minimum verdict Request changes. Hygiene findings appear in a dedicated section of the report between Build Verification and lens findings; they are author recommendations (split, add tests, review migration order) and do NOT produce Hardening Deltas (lens findings remain the source of deltas). Designed for extension to any future PR-touching skill via the PH-06 signal table contract. v0.13.0: publishes the Code Review Standard at plugins/sea/references/code-review-standard.md (CR-01..CR-08). Closes BUG_REPORT.md — a production session against honestmobile/honest-smart-sim-platform#168 showed /code-review v0.12.0 missing a build-breaking TypeScript error (hasMore referenced in JSX but never declared) that a free claude[bot] caught in seconds via tsc --noEmit. The standard makes mechanical floors mandatory: CR-01 typecheck + lint on BASE and HEAD before any lens runs; CR-02 parallel-dispatch mandatory above 200 lines / 5 files; CR-03 full-file reads on changed files >50 lines (sampling forbidden); CR-04 evidence discipline (file:line + quoted text); CR-05 severity rubric with objective conditions; CR-06 verdict computed not chosen with three auto-downgrade rules the agent cannot override (Build Verification non-empty → minimum Block; unread file → minimum Request changes; silent lens → minimum Request changes); CR-07 lens completion criteria (Quality lens must produce six structured outputs including the JSX-identifier scan that catches the PR-168 class of bug); CR-08 self-attestation in Methodology section. /code-review skill rewritten to implement CR-01..CR-08 by reference rather than restating them. Anchor case PR-168 documented in the standard for calibration tracking.
